title: Mathematics + Financial Literacy: Tamil Nadu Class 10
subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A Chennai family deposits ₹12,000 in a bank at 6% compound interest annually. What is the amount after 3 years?
questions.explanation: A = 12000 × (1.06)³ = 12000 × 1.191016 = ₹14,292 ≈ ₹14,326 (exact: 12000×1.191016 = 14292.19). Tamil Nadu's cooperative banks offer 5.5-6% on FDs. Understanding compound interest helps families make better investment decisions.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: Using statistics: Monthly expenses of a Tamil Nadu household over 6 months: ₹18,000, ₹22,000, ₹19,000, ₹25,000, ₹21,000, ₹23,000. Calculate mean, median. Is this family's spending stable?

options.text: Mean=₹21,333, Median=₹21,500; relatively stable with some variation

questions.explanation: Mean = (18000+22000+19000+25000+21000+23000)/6 = 128000/6 = ₹21,333. Sorted: 18000,19000,21000,22000,23000,25000. Median = (21000+22000)/2 = ₹21,500. Mean≈Median means relatively symmetric distribution — spending is somewhat stable. Financial planners say mean-median gap < 5% indicates stable spending patterns.
questions.subject: Mathematics
